segments.text: The burden of the valley of vision.
What aileth thee now, that thou art wholly gone up to the housetops?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: A description of the city
Thou that art full of stirs, a tumultuous city, a joyous city: thy slain men are not slain with the sword, nor dead in battle.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: All thy rulers are fled together, they are bound by the archers: all that are found in thee are bound together, which have fled from far.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Look away from me; I will weep bitterly, labour not to comfort me, because of the spoiling of the daughter of my people.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

footnotes: weep bitterly: Heb. be bitter in weeping

segments.text: For it is a day of trouble, and of treading down, and of perplexity by the Lord GOD of hosts in the valley of vision, breaking down the walls, and of crying to the mountains.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Military description
And Elam bare the quiver with chariots of men and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And it shall come to pass, that thy choicest valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall set themselves in array at the gate.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

footnotes: thy choicest valleys: Heb. the choice of thy valleys

segments.text: And he discovered the covering of Judah, and thou didst look in that day to the armour of the house of the forest.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Ye have seen also the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And ye have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and the houses have ye broken down to fortify the wall.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Ye made also a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: but ye have not looked unto the maker thereof, neither had respect unto him that fashioned it long ago.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Call to weeping, and to mourning, and to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth
segments.speaker: Lord GOD of hosts

segments.text: And behold joy and gladness, slaying oxen, and killing sheep, eating flesh, and drinking wine: let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Surely this iniquity shall not be purged from you till ye die
segments.speaker: LORD of hosts

segments.text: Go, get thee unto this treasurer, even unto Shebna, which is over the house, and say,
segments.speaker: Lord GOD of hosts

segments.text: What hast thou here? and whom hast thou here, that thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, as he that heweth him out a sepulchre on high, and that graveth an habitation for himself in a rock?
segments.speaker: Lord GOD of hosts

segments.text: Behold, the LORD will carry thee away with a mighty captivity, and will surely cover thee.
segments.speaker: LORD

footnotes: will carry thee away with a mighty captivity: or, who covered thee with an excellent covering, and clothed thee gorgeously, shall surely, etc
a mighty captivity: Heb. the captivity of a man

segments.text: He will surely violently turn and toss thee like a ball into a large country: there shalt thou die, and there the chariots of thy glory shall be the shame of thy lord's house.
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: And I will drive thee from thy station, and from thy state shall he pull thee down.
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open.
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: And I will fasten him as a nail in a sure place; and he shall be for a glorious throne to his father's house.
segments.speaker: LORD

segments.text: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house, the offspring and the issue, all vessels of small quantity, from the vessels of cups, even to all the vessels of flagons.
segments.speaker: LORD

footnotes: vessels of flagons: or, instruments of viols

segments.text: In that day, shall the nail that is fastened in the sure place be removed, and be cut down, and fall; and the burden that was upon it shall be cut off: for the LORD hath spoken it.
segments.speaker: LORD of hosts
